title: Lake Wateree Camping | South Carolina Parks Official Site
fullText: Lake Wateree Camping | South Carolina Parks Official Site Park Finder Camping & Lodging + See & Do + Education & History + Mission & Stories Programs & Events Travel Tools Newsletter Deals Reservations Park Passports Gift Cards Shop Careers Volunteers Lake Wateree DIRECTIONS 881 State Park RD Winnsboro, SC 29180 PHONE 803.482.6401 EMAIL MAKE A RESERVATION TOP 5 THINGS TO DO MAPS & BROCHURES FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS PARK ADVISORY The gas pump at Lake Wateree is currently out of order. We apologize for the inconvenience. July 4th Fireworks Advisory Visitors planning to attend the July 4th fireworks celebration should be aware of the following important updates: Park Capacity Closures:  The entrance gates will close once the park reaches parking capacity. No vehicle traffic will be allowed in until after the fireworks show concludes. Please plan to arrive early and expect delays. Boat Trailer Parking: Boat trailer parking will be extremely limited . Guests arriving with boat trailers may be turned away before the park reaches overall capacity if trailer parking is full. Boat Ramp and Dock Closure:  The boat ramp and dock will be closed starting at  9:00 PM  and will remain closed until the fireworks show is over. Parking Notice for Campers and Picnic Pad Reservations:  There will be  no designated parking available in the day-use area  for those with camping or picnic pad reservations. Guests with reservations should plan accordingly. We appreciate your cooperation and look forward to celebrating safely with you!     We are hiring Seasonal Assistant Rangers, Welcome Booth Attendants, and Retail Attendants. For more information and to apply, email us at lwatereesp@scprt.com . HOURS Sunrise to sunset, daily. OFFICE 8 a.m. - 5 p.m., daily ADMISSION $3 adults; $1.50 SC seniors; $1 children age 6-15; age 5 & younger free PETS Pets are allowed in most outdoor areas provided they are kept under physical restraint or on a leash not longer than six feet. Owners will be asked to remove noisy or dangerous pets or pets that threaten or harass wildlife. Wifi Available Introduction Photo Gallery Camping Things To Do Trails Programs & Events Area Info Unique Features Visitor Survey GET YOUR GEAR! Lake Wateree State Park Midlands Camping Camping Information: Each site is paved and has individual water and electrical hookups, and picnic table. 28 campsites have 50 amp electrical, water and sewer hookups. The campground is convenient to restroom facilities with hot showers. 50 sites can accommodate RVs up to 40 feet, others up to 35 feet. Eight sites are pull-through, while 23 are waterfront. Camping Reservations: To make a camping reservation or view current rates, call toll-free 1-866-345-PARK or visit our reservations page by clicking here . Rates vary by season and demand and are subject to change. Camping reservations must be made for a minimum of two nights. Reservations requested less than one (1) day in advance of check-in must be made directly through the park. For example: If today is Friday and you are making a reservation checking in on today, Friday, you must make the reservation by contacting the park directly. Standard Sites with Elec. & Water: 72 campsites Full Hookup Sites with Elec, Water, Sewer: 28 campsites Dump Station: Yes Primitive Group Area: The Primitive camping area is an ideal spot for organized groups up to 50. An organized group is defined as a group that holds regular meetings and has a lead officer or elected official. The area includes a water source at the primitive parking lot, and there are multiple fire rings in the primitive area with picnic tables. The nearest restroom facility is a short walk to the Taylor Creek campground bathhouse. You would leave the primitive parking area, turn right, walk up the road and turn left into Taylor Creek campground. Reservations are accepted. To make your reservation call the Tackle Shop at 803-482-6401. Minimum rate for primitive site is $15-20 per night. Tent Camping: Yes RV Camping: Yes Pets: Pets are allowed in most outdoor areas provided they are kept under physical restraint or on a leash not longer than six feet. Owners will be asked to remove noisy or dangerous pets or pets that threaten or harass wildlife. Golf Cart Policy Important Info EXPLORE MORE UPCOMING STATE PARK EVENTS SHARE YOUR EXPERIENCES ON SOCIAL MEDIA Use #SCStateParks and your content could be featured here! You can also upload your photos for us to use across South Carolina State Parks' Marketing by using the  Public Uploader  and agreeing to the  Terms & Conditions .  South Carolina State Parks Contact Us Journalists & Influencers FAQ's Recycling Careers Volunteers Rules & Regulations Make a Reservation Donate Privacy Policy Accessibility Legal South Carolina State House Sign up to receive the Newsletter DiscoverSouthCarolina.com SCPRT.com Beautiful Places Alliance Copyright 2026, South Carolina Department of Parks, Recreation & Tourism. fullText: Lake Wateree | South Carolina Parks Official Site Park Finder Camping & Lodging + See & Do + Education & History + Mission & Stories Programs & Events Travel Tools Newsletter Deals Reservations Park Passports Gift Cards Shop Careers Volunteers Lake Wateree DIRECTIONS 881 State Park RD Winnsboro, SC 29180 PHONE 803.482.6401 EMAIL MAKE A RESERVATION TOP 5 THINGS TO DO MAPS & BROCHURES FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS PARK ADVISORY The gas pump at Lake Wateree is currently out of order. We apologize for the inconvenience. No vehicle traffic will be allowed in until after the fireworks show concludes. Please plan to arrive early and expect delays. Boat Trailer Parking: Boat trailer parking will be extremely limited . For more information and to apply, email us at lwatereesp@scprt.com . HOURS Sunrise to sunset, daily. OFFICE 8 a.m. - 5 p.m., daily ADMISSION $3 adults; $1.50 SC seniors; $1 children age 6-15; age 5 & younger free PETS Pets are allowed in most outdoor areas provided they are kept under physical restraint or on a leash not longer than six feet. Wifi Available Introduction Photo Gallery Camping Things To Do Trails Programs & Events Area Info Unique Features Visitor Survey GET YOUR GEAR! Lake Wateree State Park Midlands Travis Sellers Park Manager I am Travis Sellers, manager of Lake Wateree State Park. Growing up in Conway, SC, my family and I visited Myrtle Beach State Park regularly, and many of my childhood memories took place there. We would have picnics, swim in the pool, play on the playground and enjoy each other’s company. While visiting the park, my passion for the outdoors and wildlife grew. Once I got older, I realized that being a park ranger was something that I wanted to pursue as a career. My favorite thing about Lake Wateree State Park is going out on the lake to fish and enjoy the sunset. Fishing is one of my favorite pastimes and the lake it is the perfect place to relax. **Our Ultimate Outsider stamp is located just outside the entrance to the park store. Lake Wateree fishing and boating can meet the needs of anyone looking for fun on South Carolina lakes . Numerous Lake Wateree fishing tournaments are hosted here every year, and the park provides a publicly accessible boat ramp, tackle shop and a refueling dock.  An ample number of Lake Wateree camping sites with water and electrical hookups are available. Many Lake Wateree camping sites can also accommodate large RVs. Although the lake serves as the focal point for Wateree, other day use activities are available including picnicking and a short, wooded nature trail. BY THE NUMBERS 13800 acres of Lake Wateree accessible through the park 1 boat ramp with 2 lanes and in-water refueling 100 paved campsites for RVs or tents -- 23 of which are waterfront 1 state-of-the-art playground for children to enjoy 2.3 mile Desportes Island loop trail for hiking 238 acres along the shores of Lake Wateree Golf Cart Policy Important Info EXPLORE MORE UPCOMING STATE PARK EVENTS SHARE YOUR EXPERIENCES ON SOCIAL MEDIA Use #SCStateParks and your content could be featured here! You can also upload your photos for us to use across South Carolina State Parks' Marketing by using the  Public Uploader  and agreeing to the  Terms & Conditions .  South Carolina State Parks Contact Us Journalists & Influencers FAQ's Recycling Careers Volunteers Rules & Regulations Make a Reservation Donate Privacy Policy Accessibility Legal South Carolina State House Sign up to receive the Newsletter DiscoverSouthCarolina.com SCPRT.com Beautiful Places Alliance Copyright 2026, South Carolina Department of Parks, Recreation & Tourism.
